title: HCR RNA-FISH protocol for the whole-mount brains of Drosophila and other insects
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"63lel","text":"This is a protocol to perform RNA fluorescent in situ hybridization (RNA-FISH) using hybridization chain reaction (HCR) on whole-mount samples of the brains of the fly Drosophila melanogaster and other insects, e.g. the jumping ant Harpegnathos saltator. Probes and HCR reagents are purchased from Molecular Instruments. This protocol is loosely based on the \"generic sample in solution\" protocol published by Molecular Instruments. Our modifications include the description of fixation conditions, counterstaining by Hoechst, and altered washes. Additionally, we use larger concentrations of probes and hairpins following the protocol described by Younger, Herre et al. 2020. We have successfully employed this protocol to stain insect brains with up to 4 different probe sets simultaneously (hairpins conjugated with Alexa Fluor 488, 546, 496, and 647).","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":168,"length":23},{"style":"italic","offset":232,"length":21}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":298,"length":21},{"key":1,"offset":359,"length":72},{"key":2,"offset":795,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"9rg11","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1kevi","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":3,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"9i7h","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"709B94A036A911ECBD30851F7CBC6798","url":"https://www.molecularinstruments.com/"}},"1":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"07BE124036AA11ECBD30851F7CBC6798","url":"https://files.molecularinstruments.com/MI-Protocol-RNAFISH-GenericSolution-Rev7.pdf"}},"2":{"type":"citation","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"49613CBE341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","title":"Non-canonical odor coding ensures unbreakable mosquito attraction to humans","protocol_guid":"","journal":"bioRxiv","date":"2020-11-08T23:19:00Z","created_on":"2026-02-10T22:33:37Z","doi":"https://doi.org/10.1101/2020.11.07.368720","authors":"Meg A. Younger, Margaret Herre, Alison R. Ehrlich, Zhongyan Gong, Zachary N. Gilbert, Saher Rahiel, Benjamin J. Matthews, Leslie B. Vosshall","can_edit":false}},"3":{"type":"citation","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"49613CBE341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","title":"Non-canonical odor coding ensures unbreakable mosquito attraction to humans","protocol_guid":"","journal":"bioRxiv","date":"2020-11-08T23:19:00Z","created_on":"2026-02-10T22:33:37Z","doi":"https://doi.org/10.1101/2020.11.07.368720","authors":"Meg A. Younger, Margaret Herre, Alison R. Ehrlich, Zhongyan Gong, Zachary N. Gilbert, Saher Rahiel, Benjamin J. Matthews, Leslie B. Vosshall","can_edit":false}}}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"8jdji","text":"WORKING PRACTICES:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":18}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c6ba1","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6s4bn","text":"Prepare all buffers using nuclease-free water. Use filter tips and nuclease-free tubes. If using spot plates, pre-clean them first with household bleach diluted 1:10 in water and then with 70% ethanol. Wear gloves and adhere to other practices aimed at minimizing RNA degradation in the sample. Working in a clean bench is not required if other RNAse-free practice are followed.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fu1cl","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"hjh","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"74r34","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dffht","text":"PROBE DESIGN:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":13}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f7j4","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2d2nl","text":"We select the target sequence or isoform of the gene of interest and let Molecular Instruments design the probes. For genes with multiple isoforms, either target the isoform that includes as many as possible constitutive exons and as few as possible alternatively spliced exons, or the isoform that has the highest RNA-seq coverage (assessed visually in IGV) if RNA-seq data are available. Aim for the highest number of probes in a set, ideally 40, although we have successfully performed experiments with probe sets containing \u003c20 probes.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"69l1b","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"50m9f","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e29nl","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fn5om","text":"AMPLIFIER CHOICE:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":17}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5et1h","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7v0dr","text":"We routinely perform multiplexed stainings with up to 4 different probe sets and a Hoechst counterstain. We use amplifiers conjugated with Alexa Fluor 488, 546, 594, and 647. We are able to detect clearly distinguishable signals with minimal bleed-through on our confocal microscope (Leica SP8). However, be aware that simultaneously using fluorophores with partially overlapping spectra (e.g. AF 546 and 594) requires setting narrower detection ranges, which reduces the amount of signal detected.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"4cbks","text":"This protocol uses solutions of paraformaldehyde and formamide, which are highly toxic chemicals. Consult the SDS sheets of the reagents used in this protocol for proper handling instructions.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Prepare all solutions
Pre-heat an aliquot of Probe Hybridization Buffer if proceeding with hybridization on the same day (see step case below) 37 °C
Dissect brains in cold Schneider's Medium Dissection can also be done in 1X Nuclease-Free PBS.

Fix brains in 800 μL of Fixation Buffer 00:20:00 Room temperature 24 rpm, °C, , Nutator or 60 rpm, °C, , Orbital shaker For fixation and all subsequent steps, samples can be placed either in Eppendorf tubes or in wells of a spot plate (e.g. Pyrex spot plate with 9 depressions, Catalog #CLS722085). Tubes are incubated on a nutator and plates are incubated on an orbital (horizontal) shaker.
Rinse 3x with 500 μL of PBST Room temperature
Wash 3x 15 min with 500 μL of PBST 00:15:00 00:15:00 00:15:00 Room temperature 24 rpm, °C, , Nutator or 60 rpm, °C, , Orbital shaker
Pre-hybridize samples by incubating them with 250 μL of warm Probe Hybridization Buffer from step 2 00:10:00, can be extended to 30 min 37 °C

In the meantime, prepare a 16 nM probe solution by adding 8 pmol of each probe mixture (e.g. 8 μL of 1 μM stock) to the warm Probe Hybridization Buffer for the total volume of 500 μL 4 μL of each probe in 250 μL total volume should work as well.
Remove the pre-hybridization solution from the sample and add the probe solution from step 9 Take extra care while removing the liquid. Hybridization Buffer is viscous and brains may float.
Incubate samples with the probes Overnight, We usually do ~24 h, but incubation can be extended to ~48 h. Minimum recommended is 12 h 37 °C 24 rpm, °C, , Nutator or 60 rpm, °C, , Orbital shaker
Pre-heat an aliquot of Probe Wash Buffer 37 °C
Equilibrate an aliquot of Amplification Buffer to room temperature Room temperature
Remove excess probes by washing 5x 10 min with pre-heated Probe Wash Buffer from step 12 00:10:00 00:10:00 00:10:00 00:10:00 00:10:00 37 °C 24 rpm, °C, , Nutator or 60 rpm, °C, , Orbital shaker

In the meantime, prepare separately each hairpin (h1 and h2) of each amplifier. Add 7 μL / sample of the 3 μM stock of each hairpin to a separate PCR tube. For example, if amplifiers B1 and B2 are being used, prepare 4 PCR tubes that contain B1-h1, B1-h2, B2-h1, and B2-h2, respectively. Incubate the tubes in a thermocycler at 95 ºC for 90 sec. Immediately take them out of the machine (while it is still at 95 ºC), place them in a rack and incubate them at room temperature in the dark for at least 30 min. 00:30:00, or longer Room temperature
Wash samples 2x 5 min with 500 μL of 5X SSCT 00:05:00 00:05:00 Room temperature 24 rpm, °C, , Nutator or 60 rpm, °C, , Orbital shaker
Pre-amplify each sample with 250 μL of Amplification Buffer 00:10:00, can be extended to 30 min Room temperature 24 rpm, °C, , Nutator or 60 rpm, °C, , Orbital shaker

In the meantime, add 6 μL of each hairpin from step 15 to 100 μL of Amplification Buffer (keep the volume of Amplification Buffer at 100 μL even if multiple hairpin sets are being used) Room temperature
Remove 250 μL liquid from the samples and add the Amplification Buffer + hairpins from step 18 Take extra care while removing the liquid. Amplification Buffer is viscous and the brain may float.
Incubate in the dark Overnight Room temperature 24 rpm, °C, , Nutator or 60 rpm, °C, , Orbital shaker
Equilibrate an aliquot of Probe Wash Buffer to room temperature Room temperature
Add 300 μL of 5X SSCT to samples, which at this point still contain Amplification Buffer + hairpins. This will make the solution inside the tubes less viscous. Remove as much liquid as possible.
Wash 1x 5 min with 500 μL of 5X SSCT 00:05:00 Room temperature 24 rpm, °C, , Nutator or 60 rpm, °C, , Orbital shaker
Wash 1x 15 min with 500 μL of Probe Wash Buffer 00:15:00 Room temperature 24 rpm, °C, , Nutator or 60 rpm, °C, , Orbital shaker

If not using Hoechst, skip this step and the next and proceed to step 28. If using Hoechst: incubate samples with 500 μL of Probe Wash Buffer + Hoechst (1 μL of the 5 mg/mL stock - final concentration 10 μg/mL) for 2 h 02:00:00 Room temperature 24 rpm, °C, , Nutator or 60 rpm, °C, , Orbital shaker
Wash 1x 15 min with 500 μL of Probe Wash Buffer 00:15:00 Room temperature 24 rpm, °C, , Nutator or 60 rpm, °C, , Orbital shaker
Wash 1x 10 min with 500 μL of 5X SSCT 00:10:00 Room temperature 24 rpm, °C, , Nutator or 60 rpm, °C, , Orbital shaker
Rinse with 1X Nuclease-Free PBS to remove the detergent
Remove the PBS and add Slowfade (or another antifade mountant)
If necessary, finish dissecting to remove extra tissue. Mount brains on slides.
All the next steps are light sensitive and must be done in the dark!
Proceed with imaging
Choose the version of the protocol you would like to follow (Fly is default)

Fix brains in 500 μL of Fixation Buffer Overnight, We usually do 20-24 h 4 °C 24 rpm, °C, , Nutator or 60 rpm, °C, , Orbital shaker For fixation and all subsequent steps, samples can be placed either in Eppendorf tubes or in wells of a spot plate (e.g. Pyrex spot plate with 9 depressions, Catalog #CLS722085). Tubes are incubated on a nutator and plates are incubated on an orbital (horizontal) shaker.
Wash 3x 10 min with 500 μL of PBST 00:10:00 00:10:00 00:10:00 4 °C 24 rpm, °C, , Nutator or 60 rpm, °C, , Orbital shaker
Pre-hybridize samples by incubating them with 500 μL of warm Probe Hybridization Buffer from step 12 00:30:00 37 °C
In the meantime, prepare a 16 nM probe solution by adding 8 pmol of each probe mixture (e.g. 8 μL of 1 μM stock) to the warm Probe Hybridization Buffer for the total volume of 500 μL
Remove the pre-hybridization solution from the sample and add the probe solution from step 14 Take extra care while removing the liquid. Hybridization Buffer is viscous and brains may float.
Incubate samples with the probes Overnight, We usually do 24-48 h 37 °C 24 rpm, °C, , Nutator or 60 rpm, °C, , Orbital shaker
Equilibrate an aliquot of Amplification Buffer to room temperature Room temperature
Remove excess probes by washing 5x 10 min with pre-heated Probe Wash Buffer from step 17 00:10:00 00:10:00 00:10:00 00:10:00 00:10:00 37 °C 24 rpm, °C, , Nutator or 60 rpm, °C, , Orbital shaker

In the meantime, prepare separately each hairpin (h1 and h2) of each amplifier. Add 7 μL / sample of the 3 μM stock of each hairpin to a separate PCR tube. For example, if amplifiers B1 and B2 are being used, prepare 4 PCR tubes that contain B1-h1, B1-h2, B2-h1, and B2-h2, respectively. Incubate the tubes in a thermocycler at 95 ºC for 90 sec. Immediately take them out of the machine (while it is still at 95 ºC), place them in a rack and incubate them at room temperature in the dark for at least 30 min. 00:30:00, or longer Room temperature
Wash samples 2x 5 min with 500 μL of 5X SSCT 00:05:00 00:05:00 Room temperature 24 rpm, °C, , Nutator or 60 rpm, °C, , Orbital shaker
Pre-amplify each sample with 500 μL of Amplification Buffer 00:30:00 Room temperature 24 rpm, °C, , Nutator or 60 rpm, °C, , Orbital shaker
In the meantime, add 6 μL of each hairpin to 100 μL of Amplification Buffer (keep the volume of Amplification Buffer at 100 μL even if multiple hairpin sets are being used) Room temperature
Remove liquid from the samples and add the Amplification Buffer + hairpins from step 23 Take extra care while removing the liquid. Amplification Buffer is viscous and the brain may float.
All the next steps are light sensitive and must be done in the dark!
Incubate in the dark Overnight Room temperature 24 rpm, °C, , Nutator or 60 rpm, °C, , Orbital shaker
Add 300 μL of 5X SSCT to samples, which at this point still contain Amplification Buffer + hairpins. This will make the solution inside the tubes less viscous. Remove as much liquid as possible.

Incubate samples with 500 μL of Probe Wash Buffer + Hoechst (1 μL of the 5 mg/mL stock - final concentration 10 μg/mL) for 1.5 h 01:30:00 Room temperature 24 rpm, °C, , Nutator or 60 rpm, °C, , Orbital shaker
Wash 2x 5 min with 500 μL of 5X SSCT 00:05:00 00:05:00 Room temperature 24 rpm, °C, , Nutator or 60 rpm, °C, , Orbital shaker
Mount brains on slides in Slowfade (or another antifade mountant)
Proceed with imaging
Dehydrate in a graded series of MeOH/PBST washes (add 500 μL at each step): 25% MeOH : 75% PBST 00:10:00 50% MeOH : 50% PBST 00:10:00 75% MeOH : 25% PBST 00:10:00 100% MeOH : 0% PBST 00:10:00 4 °C
Replace 100% MeOH in the tube and incubate at -20 ºC Overnight -20 °C
If desired, samples can be stored at -20 ºC long-term (for months and possibly longer).
Rehydrate in a graded series of MeOH/PBST washes (add 500 μL at each step): 75% MeOH : 25% PBST 00:10:00 50% MeOH : 50% PBST 00:10:00 25% MeOH : 75% PBST 00:10:00 0% MeOH : 100% PBST 00:10:00 4 °C
Wash 1x 10' with 500 μL of PBST 00:10:00 Room temperature
In the meantime, pre-heat an aliquot of Probe Hybridization Buffer 37 °C
Pre-heat an aliquot of Probe Wash Buffer 37 °C

Repeat step 28: Add 500 μL of Probe Wash Buffer + Hoechst (1 μL of the 5 mg/mL stock - final concentration 10 μg/mL) and incubate for 1.5 h 01:30:00 Room temperature 24 rpm, °C, , Nutator or 60 rpm, °C, , Orbital shaker Steps 28 and 29 add up to 3 h of incubation with Hoechst. In our experience, this is a minimum amount of time required for complete penetration of brains that are as large as the brain of an adult worker of Harpegnathos saltator. Still, the quality of Hoechst staining varies across experiments. In principle, incubation may be extended, but this may also increase the stringency of washes since Hoechst is diluted in 5X SSCT.
